en:
The Westhaven Express was due to leave at eight o'clock, but the storm had delayed it for hours.
Passengers who had arrived early were now stranded on the platform with nowhere to go.
A few preferred to wait in the buffet rather than stand in the cold.
Others would rather have taken a taxi home, but the roads were blocked by fallen trees.
At last an announcement was made: the train would depart at midnight if conditions improved.
By then most people had grown tired and hungry.
When the express finally pulled in, everyone rushed aboard as if they had been waiting for years.
The journey, though late, proved smoother than anyone had expected.
cn:
「西港快车」原定八点发车，暴风雨却让它延误了数小时。
早早到站的旅客被困在月台上，无处可去。
有几位宁愿在餐车等候，也不愿在寒风中站着。
还有人本想打车回家，但道路被倒下的树木堵住了。
终于广播宣布：若天气好转，列车将于午夜发车。
到那时多数人又累又饿。
快车终于进站时，大家仿佛等了好几年似的涌上车。
旅程虽迟，却比任何人预想的都顺利。

en:
For centuries scholars have argued about the purpose of Stonehenge.
Some believe it served as the first calendar ever devised by human beings.
The massive stones were arranged so that sunlight would strike certain points at solstice.
Ancient people who had no written records could still mark the seasons with precision.
Modern research has shown that the site required enormous effort to build.
Workers need not have understood astronomy to follow the plans left by their leaders.
Yet they did not need to move the stones at all if the design had been simpler.
Stonehenge remains proof that early societies measured time long before clocks existed.
cn:
数个世纪以来，学者一直在争论巨石阵的用途。
有人认为它是人类设计的第一部历法。
巨石排列得使阳光在至日照到特定位置。
没有文字记录的古人仍能精确标记季节。
现代研究表明，建造该遗址需要巨大劳力。
劳工不必懂天文学，只要按首领留下的方案施工即可。
但若设计更简单，他们本不必搬运这些石头。
巨石阵证明：早在钟表出现之前，早期社会已在计量时间。

en:
The patient visited his doctor for the third time that month, convinced that something was seriously wrong.
He pointed out that he felt dizzy every morning and that his heart beat too fast.
The doctor noticed that he looked pale but found nothing abnormal in the examination.
She realized that he was worrying about illnesses he did not have.
"There is nothing to worry about," she said gently. "You are perfectly healthy."
He insisted that he had read about rare diseases on the internet.
The doctor explained that reading medical articles could frighten anyone.
At last he left the surgery, still uneasy but slightly reassured.
cn:
这位病人当月第三次来看医生，坚信自己得了重病。
他指出自己每天早晨头晕、心跳过快。
医生注意到他脸色苍白，但检查未发现异常。
她意识到他在为并不存在的病担忧。
「没什么可担心的，」她温和地说，「你非常健康。」
他坚持说自己在网上读过罕见疾病的介绍。
医生解释说，读医学文章足以吓坏任何人。
他终于离开诊所，仍不安，却稍感宽慰。

en:
Two brothers who looked exactly alike worked in the same office and confused everyone.
Staff often asked themselves whether they were speaking to Henry or to James.
One morning Henry introduced himself to a new colleague, who smiled and said, "We met yesterday."
Henry laughed and explained that the man had met his brother instead.
The colleague felt foolish until James appeared and the two stood side by side.
From that day on, each brother wore a different tie so that people could tell them apart.
They even labelled their desks to avoid mixing up their work.
The office learned that identical twins must help others identify themselves.
cn:
一对长相一模一样的兄弟在同一办公室工作，把大家搞糊涂了。
员工常暗自琢磨正在说话的是亨利还是詹姆斯。
一天早上亨利向新同事自我介绍，对方笑着说：「我们昨天见过。」
亨利笑着解释，那人昨天见到的是他的兄弟。
同事自觉尴尬，直到詹姆斯出现，两人并肩而立。
从那天起，兄弟俩戴上不同领带，好让人分辨。
他们甚至给办公桌贴标签，以免弄混工作。
办公室明白了：同卵双胞胎得帮别人认出自己。

en:
While clearing the attic, an old man found a box of letters he had written fifty years ago.
He sat by the window and read them one by one, lost in memories of youth.
He wished he were young again, though he knew that was impossible.
He wished he had told her how he felt before she moved abroad.
The handwriting seemed strange, as if it belonged to another person.
For a moment he imagined the past could return, but the illusion soon faded.
He closed the box and sighed, accepting that love remembered is not love restored.
The letters remained a treasure, yet they could not change the present.
cn:
老人清理阁楼时发现一盒五十年前写的信。
他坐在窗边一封封读起，沉浸在青春回忆里。
他希望自己再度年轻，尽管明知不可能。
他后悔当年没在她出国前表白心意。
字迹显得陌生，仿佛属于另一个人。
有一瞬间他幻想过去能重来，幻觉很快消散。
他合上箱子叹了口气，接受被记住的爱无法复还。
这些信仍是珍宝，却不能改变当下。

en:
My nephew is not very musical, yet he insists on practising the trumpet every evening.
The noise is so dreadful that the whole family suffers.
Last week I had my windows repaired after the vibrations cracked the glass.
My sister had her ears tested, though the doctor found nothing wrong with her hearing.
We finally had a long talk with the boy and suggested lessons with a proper teacher.
He agreed, and his playing improved a little, but not enough for our taste.
Now we have had soundproof panels fitted to his bedroom door.
Peace has returned, and the trumpet is heard only when we choose to listen.
cn:
我侄子没什么音乐天分，却坚持每晚练小号。
噪音难听得全家受罪。
上周震动把玻璃震裂，我找人修好了窗户。
我妹妹去检查了听力，医生说她听觉正常。
我们最终和男孩长谈，建议跟正规老师学。
他答应了，演奏稍有进步，仍不合我们胃口。
现在我们给卧室门装了隔音板。
安宁恢复了，小号声只有在我们愿意听时才传来。

en:
The explorers prepared for months before attempting to fly over the South Pole in a balloon.
They debated whether to carry extra fuel or to reduce weight by leaving supplies behind.
Weather reports warned that storms could appear without warning above the ice.
On the chosen day the balloon rose steadily into the clear Antarctic sky.
For hours they drifted over endless white, whether looking down or ahead.
Instruments showed they had crossed the pole at last.
A sudden gust threatened to tear the fabric, but the pilot kept control.
They landed safely on the far side, exhausted but triumphant.
cn:
探险者筹备数月，才尝试乘气球飞越南极。
他们争论是携带额外燃料，还是减轻负重、少带补给。
气象报告警告：冰原上空风暴可能毫无征兆袭来。
选定之日，气球稳稳升入南极晴朗的天空。
数小时里，无论俯视还是远眺，都是无尽白雪。
仪器显示他们终于跨越了极点。
一阵狂风几乎撕裂气囊，飞行员仍控制住局面。
他们在另一侧安全着陆，精疲力竭却凯旋。

en:
Two students entered the forest to study birds and soon lost their way among the tall trees.
They had better have marked their path, but neither had brought a map.
One would rather turn back, yet the other insisted on going deeper.
As darkness fell, they realized they had wandered for hours.
At last they found a stream and followed it, hoping it would lead to a road.
A ranger who had been searching for them appeared at dawn.
He told them they had better not enter the forest alone again without a compass.
They thanked him and promised to prepare properly next time.
cn:
两名学生进森林观鸟，很快在参天大树间迷了路。
他们本最好沿路做标记，却谁也没带地图。
一人宁愿折返，另一人却坚持往深处走。
天色渐暗，他们意识到自己已徘徊数小时。
终于发现一条小溪，沿溪而行，指望找到大路。
黎明时分，一直在搜寻他们的护林员出现了。
他告诉他们，下次没有指南针最好不要独自进森林。
他们道谢，并保证下次做好准备。

en:
A shop assistant dreamed that he had stolen money from the till and woke in a panic.
He felt so guilty that he could hardly face his employer.
Such was his distress that he confessed everything the next morning.
The manager listened in surprise and checked the accounts carefully.
Not a penny was missing; the dream had been pure invention.
The assistant was so relieved that he laughed at himself.
His conscience was clear at last, though he had suffered for nothing.
He learned that imaginary guilt can feel as heavy as real wrongdoing.
cn:
一名店员梦见自己从收银机偷钱，惊醒时惊慌失措。
他内疚得几乎不敢见老板。
他痛苦之极，翌晨和盘托出。
经理惊讶地听完，仔细核对账目。
分文不少；梦境纯属臆造。
店员如释重负，自嘲一笑。
他的良心终于清白，尽管白受罪一场。
他懂得了：想象中的罪过也可以像真做错事一样沉重。

en:
Despite his fear of flying, a businessman had to attend a meeting in another city.
He bought the most expensive ticket, hoping comfort would calm his nerves.
In spite of the luxurious seat, he gripped the armrest throughout the short flight.
Turbulence shook the cabin, and he closed his eyes until the plane landed.
The journey lasted only forty minutes, yet it felt like hours.
When he arrived, colleagues joked that he looked paler than the clouds.
He admitted that no amount of money could make him enjoy flying.
Next time he would take the train, however long the trip might be.
cn:
尽管怕坐飞机，一位商人仍须赴另一城市开会。
他买了最贵的票，指望舒适能镇定神经。
尽管座位豪华，短短航程里他始终紧抓扶手。
颠簸摇晃客舱，他闭眼直到飞机落地。
飞行仅四十分钟，却仿佛过了几小时。
抵达后同事笑他比云还苍白。
他承认，再多钱也无法让他享受飞行。
下次无论多久，他都要改乘火车。

en:
The old pub was said to be haunted by a ghost who was always thirsty.
Landlords over the years had found glasses moved and beer disappearing overnight.
A great deal of beer had been wasted, according to the latest owner.
A large number of customers came to see the ghost for themselves.
One night a journalist hid in the cellar with a camera and plenty of patience.
At midnight he heard a sigh and saw a misty figure reach for a mug.
He took a photograph, but the image showed only an empty bar.
The ghost, if there was one, preferred drink to fame.
cn:
这家老酒馆据说闹鬼，鬼魂总是口渴。
历年店主都发现杯子被挪动、啤酒一夜消失。
据最新店主说，大量啤酒白白浪费。
许多顾客前来一睹鬼魂真容。
一天夜里，记者带着相机和足够耐心躲在酒窖。
午夜他听见叹息，见一团雾影伸手拿杯。
他拍了照，影像里却只有空荡吧台。
那鬼魂——若真有——宁要酒喝，不要名声。

en:
A patient sat nervously in the dentist's chair, unsure what the doctor wanted to tell him.
The dentist examined the tooth and asked whether he had felt any pain recently.
The patient said he hadn't, hadn't he been lucky?
The dentist replied that the tooth need not have been removed at all.
He explained that he had extracted the wrong one by mistake.
The patient could hardly believe what he was hearing.
"Did you want to tell me something?" he asked at last.
The dentist apologized and promised to put matters right the same day.
cn:
病人紧张地坐在牙科椅上，不知医生想说什么。
牙医检查了牙齿，问他最近是否疼痛。
病人说没有，难道不算幸运吗？
牙医回答说，那颗牙根本不必拔。
他解释说，自己误拔了另一颗。
病人几乎不敢相信自己听到的。
「你是不是想告诉我什么？」他终于问道。
牙医道歉，并承诺当天把事情纠正。
